frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Maa ke haath ke simple varan-bhaat and poli-bhaji also had a different warmth, because she made it daily with full prem and never counted it as burden. Nowadays everyone wants convenience, but a home truly feels like home only when the lady of the house puts that same mann and sanskar into feeding the family. Meri Aai managed all this without complaint, that is real devotion.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment sentimentalizes a mother's cooking and self-sacrifice while erasing it as labor ('never counted it as burden'). It also sets an implicit expectation that the 'lady of the house' should reproduce this unpaid domestic devotion, which encodes the frame's comparison mechanism.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
